title: Homeland Security Project Focuses on Immigration and Borders, Threats and Resiliency
summary: Under the leadership of the Belfer Center's Juliette Kayyem, the Homeland Security Project will focus on the challenges in protecting the U.S.
body: Led by Belfer Lecturer in International Security and former Department of Homeland Security Assistant Secretary Juliette Kayyem , the Belfer Center’s new Homeland Security Project focuses on the unique challenges and choices around protecting the United States. The project provides a forum for academic and public policy pursuits related to immigration and borders, American resiliency issues, and the future of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). “From terror to natural disaster to pandemics, homeland security is at the intersection of all evolving threats and hazards with the traditional governmental and civil responsibilities of emergency response, law enforcement, customs, border control, and immigration,” Kayyem said. The project’s inaugrial fellows include Lisa Monaco , former assistant to President Obama for homeland security and counterterrorism; Alan Bersin , former assistant secretary for international affairs and chief diplomatic officer with DHS, and former commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection; and John Carlin , former assistant attorney general for national security. “These talented individuals, who have been working at the center of our nation’s security, will provide experience and insight into exploring the challenges connected with keeping our nation safe,” Kayyem said. “I look forward to their contributions as we tackle these issues with our faculty, staff, and students.” The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "Homeland Security Project Focuses on Immigration and Borders, Threats and Resiliency." Belfer Center Newsletter. Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. (Spring 2017). result.main_content_en: The Belfer Center's new Homeland Security Project, led by former DHS Assistant Secretary Juliette Kayyem, focuses on protecting the United States by addressing unique challenges at the intersection of evolving threats. The project serves as a forum for academic and policy work related to immigration and border security, American resiliency, and the future of the Department of Homeland Security. Kayyem emphasizes that homeland security encompasses threats from terrorism to natural disasters and pandemics, linking them to government functions like emergency response, law enforcement, customs, and border control. The project's inaugural fellows include high-profile former officials such as Lisa Monaco, Alan Bersin, and John Carlin, who bring extensive experience from the center of national security. Their insights will be crucial in exploring security challenges alongside faculty, staff, and students.
result.main_content_zh: 哈佛大学贝尔弗中心新成立的国土安全项目，由前国土安全部助理部长朱丽叶·凯耶姆领导，旨在应对保护美国所面临的独特挑战。该项目为与移民和边境安全、美国复原力问题以及国土安全部未来相关的学术和公共政策研究提供了一个论坛。凯耶姆指出，国土安全处于从恐怖主义到自然灾害和流行病等所有不断演变的威胁与危险，同应急响应、执法、海关、边境管制和移民等传统政府及民间职责的交汇点。项目的首批研究员包括奥巴马总统的前国土安全及反恐助理丽莎·蒙娜可、前国土安全部国际事务助理部长兼首席外交官及前美国海关和边境保护局局长艾伦·伯辛，以及前国家安全助理司法部长约翰·卡林。这些曾在国家安全核心领域工作的资深人士将提供经验和见解，帮助探索与维护国家安全相关的挑战。
